summary: The bug is real: EndeavorDependency rows never reach the scheduling engine on any code path. The scheduler's only dependency input is UnifiedScheduleItem.dependencies (unified-scheduler.ts:113), which scheduler-converters.ts populates exclusively from TaskStep.dependsOn and Task.dependencies JSON. Neither the desktop input pipeline (task.getAll → useTaskStore → storeConnector → useSchedulerStore.computeSchedule) nor the server-side scheduler procedures (task.getNextScheduled, task.getFullSchedule) ever query the endeavorDependency table — a grep for "endeavor" in unified-scheduler.ts, scheduler-converters.ts, and scheduler-priority.ts returns zero hits. This violates the documented contract: both prisma/schema.prisma (EndeavorDependency.isHardBlock comment) and src/shared/types.ts:263 say "true = blocks scheduler, false = warning only". The deep work board does NOT respect endeavor links either (its edges come only from dependsOn/task.dependencies); the only surfaces that honor them are the endeavor graph view's "Work on Next Step" (findNextUnblockedStep) and the blocked badges in EndeavorDetail/graph layout. Resolution semantics confirmed: the blocking side is always a step (a workflow resolves to its last step per spatialScene.resolveBlockingStep), the blocked side is a task OR a step.
rootCause: The scheduler consumes only per-item `dependencies: string[]` (src/shared/unified-scheduler.ts:113), built by convertToUnifiedItems from step.dependsOn (src/shared/scheduler-converters.ts:142) and task.dependencies/dependsOn (scheduler-converters.ts:237, 334-346). scheduleForDisplay (unified-scheduler.ts:382-408) → resolveDependencies (:2010) → allocateToWorkBlocks/areDependenciesSatisfied (:1284-1313) all operate solely on that array. No hydration path folds EndeavorDependency in: (1) Desktop: task.getAll formats rows parsing only Task.dependencies JSON (src/server/router/task.ts:144) and TaskStep.dependsOn JSON (task.ts:147); storeConnector feeds tasks/workflows to the scheduler store (src/renderer/store/storeConnector.ts:101-148); computeSchedule passes only tasks/sequencedTasks/workPatterns/workSettings into ScheduleContext (src/renderer/store/useSchedulerStore.ts:178-206). (2) Server: task.getNextScheduled (task.ts:463-551) and task.getFullSchedule (task.ts:~624-689) fetch only task.findMany + TaskStep before running the same scheduler. (3) ScheduleContext itself has no endeavor field (unified-scheduler.ts:338-348). Meanwhile endeavor.addDependency (src/server/router/endeavor.ts:478-490) and spatialScene.linkWorkflows (src/server/router/spatialScene.ts:482-531) write ONLY an EndeavorDependency row — they never touch dependsOn — so those links are invisible to the scheduler by construction. The schema comment "isHardBlock … true = blocks scheduler" (prisma/schema.prisma EndeavorDependency model, ~line 537) and src/shared/types.ts:263 document the intended behavior; nothing implements it. By contrast, findNextUnblockedStep (src/shared/next-unblocked-step.ts:67-101) correctly checks isHardBlock + blockingStepStatus — but it's used only by the endeavor graph's CreateScheduleBlockButton, not the engine.

files.detail: Engine. Dependency input is item.dependencies only (:113); scheduleForDisplay :382-408 converts+resolves; areDependenciesSatisfied :1284-1313; resolveDependencies auto-heal strips deps not in itemIds/completedItemIds (:2010-2070) — injected endeavor deps must be pre-resolved or they'd be silently stripped. ScheduleContext :338-348 has no endeavor field; zero 'endeavor' mentions in file.
Builds UnifiedScheduleItem.dependencies solely from step.dependsOn (:142) and task.dependencies/dependsOn (:237, :334-346). Natural home for a new pure applyEndeavorDependencies() helper.

Hydration: formatTask parses dependencies JSON (:144) and step dependsOn (:147). Server-side scheduler runs: getNextScheduled (:463-551) and getFullSchedule (~:624-689) fetch only Task+TaskStep — no endeavorDependency query.
Desktop scheduler invocation: computeSchedule builds context from tasks/sequencedTasks/workPatterns/workSettings only (:178-206); scheduler.scheduleForDisplay(:206).
Feeds scheduler inputs from useTaskStore (:101-148, setInputs :148) — no endeavor dependency feed exists.

addDependency creates ONLY the EndeavorDependency row (:478-490), never dependsOn; getDependencies (:510) resolves blockingStepStatus — the shape needed for scheduler injection. Note these are protectedProcedure, not session-validated.

Resolution semantics: resolveBlockingStep (:876-900) — step node blocks with its own step, workflow blocks with its LAST step, standalone task can't block; resolveBlockedEndpoint (:903-914) — blocked side is a step OR a whole task. linkWorkflows (:482-531) writes only EndeavorDependency.

The ONE place hard blocks are enforced (:67-101): isHardBlock && blockingStepStatus not completed/skipped → blocked; checks dep.blockedStepId === step.id || dep.blockedTaskId === task.id. Reference semantics for the scheduler fix. Used only by graph view CreateScheduleBlockButton.tsx:40.

Deep work board comparison: deriveEdgesFromNodes (:130-181) uses only step.dependsOn + task.dependencies; computeActionableNodeIds (:189-245) computes 'unblocked' from those edges — endeavor links are NOT respected here either.
Server edge derivation also dependsOn-only (:179-210). promoteToEndeavor (:916-966) mirrors cross-workflow dependsOn into EndeavorDependency rows — dual representation of the same edge.
EndeavorDependencyWithNames carries blockingStepStatus (:276); :263 documents 'isHardBlock: true = blocks scheduler, false = warning only' — the violated contract.
EndeavorDependency model (~:526-549): blockedTaskId? XOR blockedStepId?, blockingStepId (always a step), blockingTaskId, isHardBlock @default(true) commented 'true = blocks scheduler'.

proposedFix: Inject endeavor dependencies at the engine's conversion layer (ONE-engine rule: extend the existing scheduler, don't fork blocking logic). (1) Add an optional `endeavorDependencies?: SchedulerEndeavorDependency[]` field to ScheduleContext (src/shared/unified-scheduler.ts:338) with shape { blockedTaskId?, blockedStepId?, blockingStepId, blockingTaskId, isHardBlock, blockingStepStatus } — mirroring EndeavorDependencyWithNames so callers pre-resolve blocking-step status (avoids the auto-heal trap). (2) Add a pure, exported `applyEndeavorDependencies(activeItems, completedItemIds, deps)` in src/shared/scheduler-converters.ts that: skips isHardBlock=false (soft = warning only, per types.ts:263); skips deps whose blockingStepStatus is completed/skipped (satisfied — same predicate as next-unblocked-step.ts:73-74); resolves the blocked side — blockedStepId → that unified item; blockedTaskId → the standalone task item, or for a workflow its ENTRY steps (items with workflowId === blockedTaskId and empty dependsOn — intra-workflow chaining propagates the block); then, if blockingStepId ∈ itemIds ∪ completedItemIds, appends blockingStepId to item.dependencies (deduped) so topological sort, areDependenciesSatisfied (:1284-1313), and dependency-wait timing all work unchanged; if the blocking step is incomplete but absent from the schedule set (blocking workflow not in sprint), mark the blocked items isBlocked=true and exclude them from allocation with a warning — otherwise resolveDependencies' auto-heal (:2045-2070) would silently strip the hard block. (3) Call it in scheduleForDisplay immediately after convertToUnifiedItems (unified-scheduler.ts:389), reading context.endeavorDependencies. (4) Hydrate at the three call sites: server — task.getNextScheduled and task.getFullSchedule fetch endeavorDependency.findMany({ where: { Endeavor: { sessionId: ctx.sessionId } } }) plus a single taskStep.findMany over blockingStepIds for statuses; desktop — load session-wide endeavor dependencies (extend useEndeavorStore / a new endeavor.getAllDependencies-style sessionProcedure) and pass them through storeConnector → useSchedulerStore.setInputs → computeSchedule context. Safe because: the context field is optional (all existing callers/tests unaffected when absent), the helper is pure and additive, no schema migration is needed, and blocking semantics exactly mirror the already-shipped findNextUnblockedStep predicate.
testPlan: Vitest, alongside the existing scheduler dependency suite at src/shared/__tests__/unified-scheduler-dependencies.test.ts (or a new unified-scheduler-endeavor-deps.test.ts; run via mcp__diagnostic__run_test_file). Engine-level regressions against scheduleForDisplay with context.endeavorDependencies set: (a) hard block, blockedTaskId = standalone task, incomplete blocking step in another scheduled workflow → blocked task's startTime is after the blocking step's endTime; (b) blockedTaskId = workflow → its entry steps are ordered after the blocking step; (c) blockedStepId variant; (d) blockingStepStatus 'completed'/'skipped' → no ordering constraint; (e) isHardBlock=false → no constraint; (f) blocking workflow absent from item set + incomplete blocker → blocked item lands in unscheduled (not silently auto-healed into the schedule); (g) no endeavorDependencies in context → identical output to today (back-compat). Pure-helper unit tests for applyEndeavorDependencies cover dedupe and entry-step resolution. Router-level: extend src/server/router/__tests__/task.test.ts — seed an EndeavorDependency between two sprint workflows and assert task.getNextScheduled never returns the blocked task/step before the blocking step completes, then flip the blocking step to completed and assert it does.
adjacentFindings: Deep work board 'unblocked' computation also ignores EndeavorDependency: edges derive only from step.dependsOn/task.dependencies (renderer useDeepWorkBoardStore.ts:130-181; server deepWorkBoard.ts:179-210) and computeActionableNodeIds (useDeepWorkBoardStore.ts:189-245) reads only those edges — a task hard-blocked by an endeavor link still shows as actionable on the board. Same class of bug on a second execution surface; the fix's pure helper semantics could be reused there.

Dual representation of cross-workflow edges: deepWorkBoard.promoteToEndeavor mirrors cross-workflow dependsOn into EndeavorDependency rows (deepWorkBoard.ts:916-966), so those edges block the scheduler only via the dependsOn copy; conversely endeavor.addDependency / spatialScene.linkWorkflows create only the EndeavorDependency (no dependsOn). Removing one representation (e.g. graph-view removeDependency) leaves the other in force — edge semantics are inconsistent across surfaces.

endeavor.addDependency / removeDependency / getDependencies are protectedProcedure, not sessionProcedure, and do not validate that the referenced endeavor/task/step ids belong to the caller's session (endeavor.ts:450-516); removeDependency deletes any dependency by raw id. Contradicts the 'server is the trust boundary' rule in the visionOS guidelines (spatialScene.reassignLink was hardened for exactly this).

resolveDependencies auto-heal (unified-scheduler.ts:2020-2070) silently strips any dependency id not present in the item set or completedItemIds (logged as warn + surfaced as conflict). Any future naive injection of cross-task deps will be eaten by this path unless the blocker is pre-resolved — worth a doc comment on stripInvalidDependencies.

useSchedulerStore.extractNextScheduledItem and task.getNextScheduled duplicate the same work-item filtering/skip-index logic (useSchedulerStore.ts:236-328 vs task.ts:553-616) — a drift risk; the endeavor-dep fix must touch both unless the extraction is consolidated into a shared utility.
